[[Tristan Sailor]] (cousin) }} }} '''Jacob Gagai''' (born 20 December 1995) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== He is the younger brother of [[Newcastle Knights]] player, [[Dane Gagai]].{{Cite web |date=2022-03-05 |title=Jacob Gagai |url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/players/nsw-cup/south-sydney-rabbitohs-nsw/jacob-gagai |access-date=2024-07-04 |website=New South Wales Rugby League |language=en}} He is of [[Indigenous Australian]] descent from the [[Badu people|Badu]] and [[Yam Island]] people.{{Cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2024-indigenous-jersey-reveiled|title=Rabbitohs Launch 2024 Indigenous Jersey Embodying Warrior Spirit|website=South Sydney Rabbitohs|date=9 May 2024}} Growing up he also played rugby union. ==Playing career== ===South Sydney Rabbitohs=== Gagai made his first grade debut for South Sydney against [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] in round 1 of the [[2024 NRL season]] at the [[Allegiant Stadium]] in Las Vegas. Gagai scored a try in South Sydney's 36-24 loss.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/mar/03/nrl-in-las-vegas-sea-eagles-vs-rabbitohs-broncos-v-roosters-live-updates-start-times-ticket-sales-scores-local-time-allegiant-stadium/|title=Roosters & Sea Eagles victorious – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}} In round 8 of the 2024 NRL season, Gagai scored two tries for South Sydney in their 54-20 loss against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2024-04-25/nrl-anzac-day-warriors-titans-dragons-roosters-storm-rabbitohs/103764148/|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs' Anzac Day loss to Melbourne Storm made worse by Taane Milne's 'brain explosion' on Cameron Munster|website=www.abc.net.au}} In round 18, Gagai scored two tries for South Sydney in their 32-16 victory over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-round-18-live-parramatta-eels-v-south-sydney-rabbitohs-20240704-p5jr1f.html/|title=Moses frustration hits boiling point as Eels sink to fourth-straight loss|website=www.smh.com.au}} The following week, he scored a [[hat-trick]] in South Sydney's 36-28 loss against the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/rabbitohs-go-down-to-dolphins-in-see-sawing-affair-at-kayo-stadium/|title=Rabbitohs go Down to Dolphins in See-Sawing Affair at Kayo Stadium|website=www.rabbitohs.com.au}} Gagai played 17 games for the club in the 2024 season and scored nine tries as they finished 16th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-south-sydney-rabbitohs-season-review-latrell-mitchell-20240907-p5k8qx.html/|title=The Mole's end of season review: What went wrong at the South Sydney Rabbitohs?|website=www.nine.com.au}} On 23 October 2024, Gagai was told his services were no longer required by the South Sydney club.{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/the-mole-news-2024-jacob-gagai-south-sydney-rabbitohs-contract-20241024-p5kkwx.html/|title=The Mole: 'Unlucky' South Sydney rookie Jacob Gagai cut by Bunnies despite leading club's try scoring|website=www.nine.com.au}} ===Huddersfield Giants=== On 14 November 2024, it was announced that Gagai had signed a two-year contract with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite web|title=South Sydney outside back Gagai completes super league move|URL=https://www.totalrl.com/south-sydney-rabbitohs-outside-back-jacob-gagai-completes-super-league-move|access-date=14 November 2024|work=totalrl.com}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn8gjrl9rpdo|title=Huddersfield sign utility back Gagai|publisher=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 November 2024}} Gagai played 26 games for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- |2024 |{{leagueicon|South Sydney|16}} [[South Sydney
